body_txt: Two characters may start off with nearly identical designs, if not a Palette Swap of each other, or at the least seem to be the exact same character . Over time Character Development and Art Evolution allows them to develop their own identity and visual design. An example would be a pair of female Always Identical Twins end up becoming known as Polar Opposite Twins , despite normally looking identical one changes her hair to jet black and wears jeans while the other loves to frill up their blonde hair and wear colorful dresses . Named after the biological phenomenon known as Divergent Evolution , where two closely related species develop wildly different traits over time. This is extremely common in video games, where characters who were originally identical to the lead (usually to allow for two-player play, or just because the lead is popular ) are often given their own special abilities and personality quirks in later games. It can also happen in crossovers when two characters that were very similar in their own series are allowed to interact with each other . Related to Derivative Differentiation . See also Cast Speciation , Not So Similar , Replacement Flat Character , Mirror Character , Moveset Clone . Compare Characterization Marches On . May involve a Characterisation Click Moment . For in-universe examples, see Duplicate Divergence and Twin Desynch .
